task_title: Attack actual resolvent absolute continuity and ABLM-Dirichlet identification
notes: The core identification with the BZ Dirichlet-form construction is solid under the stipulated Dirichlet ABLM extension, and it proves ν-invariance plus resolvent AC quasi-everywhere. The remaining bottleneck is exactly removal of the BZ exceptional set. A proof of instantaneous exceptional-set avoidance, resolvent strong Feller, or a uniform Lp-density bound for regularized resolvents would complete uniqueness of invariant measures.

task_title: Conditional Gibbs uniqueness theorem and sign check
notes: The conditional Gibbs/Dirichlet-form uniqueness route is complete. To apply it to the original singular SPDE, one still needs to prove that the actual ABLM semigroup is the symmetric nu-invariant semigroup associated with the closed form and to prove the pointwise resolvent absolute-continuity input.

task_title: Remove the BZ exceptional set or prove invariant measures avoid it
notes: The exceptional-set gap remains unless one proves a pointwise capacity-smoothing estimate, full transition/resolvent absolute continuity, resolvent strong Feller, or uniform L^p density bounds for the regularized semigroups. Any of these would complete the chain to uniqueness via t1.

task_title: Uniform smoothing from smooth approximants to force invariant measures absolutely continuous
notes: The smooth-approximation strategy is reduced to a sharp missing compact-local heat-kernel estimate, equivalently a uniform diagonal or L^p density bound for P_t^n(x,·). Bounded potentials provide uniform invariant-measure equivalence, log-Sobolev, spectral, and averaged Hilbert-Schmidt estimates, but not the deterministic-starting-point uniform integrability needed to prove μ<<ν.

task_title: Invariant-measure Fokker-Planck and logarithmic-derivative route
notes: The direct stationarity route reduces uniqueness to a sharp missing regularity statement: either prove stationary laws have the BZ local-time logarithmic derivative with zero current, or prove an FPK uniqueness/absolute-continuity theorem for this measure-valued gradient drift. Existing H-valued drift theorems do not cover the delta_0(u) term.

task_title: Direct deterministic-start smoothing: densities, capacity, last-noise, or skew transform
notes: The frontier is reduced to a finite-dimensional projected L^p density estimate uniform in the approximation index n and projection dimension m. Proving that estimate by Malliavin calculus would require exploiting cancellations in b_n' beyond current ABLM drift-increment bounds; proving it by heat-kernel methods would require a strict dimension-free Mehler-type comparison for bounded Gibbs perturbations.

task_title: Doob/t0-regularity route for the BV-potential skew heat equation
notes: The Doob route is reduced to proving pairwise non-singularity of positive-time transition laws. The most economical remaining estimates are either a uniform projective Hellinger/overlap bound for approximating projected laws, or uniform L1 integrability/entropy of deterministic-start fixed-time densities with respect to nu. These are weaker than full Lp density bounds and would complete uniqueness.

task_title: Projected drift/local-time estimates for finite-dimensional density bounds
notes: Projected local-time drifts are controllable uniformly in n at every fixed finite dimension, but their Fourier energy has unavoidable sqrt(m) growth. This proves useful fixed-m absolute continuity but shows that a pure finite-dimensional Girsanov-energy method cannot supply the dimension-free projective Lp estimate needed for full deterministic-start smoothing.

task_title: Last-noise fixed-time quasi-invariance and terminal Cameron-Martin smoothing
notes: The last-noise decomposition successfully proves uniform terminal Cameron-Martin smoothing of the drift convolution, with sharp scale epsilon^{3/4}. This is not sufficient for fixed-time absolute continuity because the shift is anticipating relative to the terminal Gaussian. The remaining useful target is either a uniform innovation-level Ramer/quasi-invariance theorem controlling the b_n' linearization, or the weaker compact-local Hellinger overlap condition for last-step kernels.

task_title: Low-mode controlled generalized/asymptotic coupling from projected local-time estimates
notes: The finite-dimensional estimates from t8 successfully support a finite-horizon stopped generalized coupling and explain why high modes can be made small. They also recover full asymptotic coupling for each smooth approximant. For the singular limit, the remaining bottleneck is a cancellation estimate for Pi_m(delta_0(u)-delta_0(v)) when u and v are close; separate O(sqrt(m)) bounds on Pi_m delta_0(u) and Pi_m delta_0(v) are not enough because they lead to infinite Girsanov energy over infinite time.

task_title: Local-time drift-difference cancellation for low-mode asymptotic coupling
notes: The clean Gaussian model gives exactly the desired cancellation, uniformly in n, with sharp squared exponent alpha=1. This would be sufficient for summable infinite-time Girsanov energy if it held conditionally along the coupling. The main remaining obstacle is dependence: close adapted perturbations can change local time by O(1). Thus the missing SPDE ingredient is a conditional fresh-noise/non-flattening estimate or an equivalent two-field rectangular density-increment bound for (u_t,v_t), not another one-sided local-time moment bound.

task_title: Compact-local Hellinger overlap for last-step kernels
notes: The compact-local Hellinger condition is reduced to a sharp one-sided anti-concentration estimate relative to the linear OU last-step law, equivalently to a uniform L^p bound on d gamma_epsilon^v / dR_epsilon^n(v). This estimate would follow from a terminal-innovation Ramer theorem with uniform inverse-Jacobian moments or from a dimension-free Harnack/lower heat-kernel theorem for bounded perturbations of the Gaussian Dirichlet form. The remaining obstruction is the signed b_n' derivative-local-time term in the terminal innovation Jacobian.

task_title: Lift Brownian-bridge graph-shift cancellation to the coupled SPDE via fresh-noise decomposition
notes: The fresh-noise program works exactly for conditionally independent graph shifts and for the frozen recent-noise approximation. The obstruction is now sharply localized: one needs a uniform two-field rectangular density-increment or non-flattening estimate for the actual recent stochastic flow, controlling the effect of the b_n'-dependent recent drift on zero-level densities. The t8 one-field local-time estimates are insufficient for this.

task_title: Bounded-potential anti-concentration theorem or counterexample for last-step kernels
notes: The general bounded-potential anti-concentration route from t12 is false. Bounded oscillation and invariant-measure equivalence alone cannot yield the needed compact-local Hellinger overlap. For the actual potentials Psi_{n,m}, the problem is not disproved: their extra linewise bounded-variation/local-time structure must be exploited by a sharper argument.

task_title: Order-preserving / monotone random dynamical systems route to uniqueness
notes: The monotone route gives a clear finite-time exact-coupling contraction mechanism in L1: force the lower ordered solution positive on the interior, then heat flow contracts the order interval and only boundary-layer local time remains. The remaining gap is an infinite-time recurrence/density estimate for such bulk-positive contraction blocks, or a no-reexpansion estimate for ordered intervals. Proving that conditional block-synchronization criterion would establish uniqueness without any Girsanov or pointwise absolute-continuity argument.

task_title: Exploit ridge-average and linewise-BV structure for last-step anti-concentration
notes: The ridge route is reduced to a finite/noncommuting membrane insertion theorem: adding positive affine half-space membrane jumps of total mass 1 should lower transition kernels by only a constant depending on that mass, not on the number of membranes, orientations, n, or m. This theorem is proved in the one-ridge and commuting cases and, together with trace-class OU time comparison, would close the compact-local Hellinger bottleneck.

task_title: Final attack on monotone synchronization via recurrent positive-bulk contraction blocks
notes: The monotone route is reduced to one sharp missing estimate: after the ordered pair has been made L1-small, the positive part of the signed local-time drift difference must have arbitrarily small asymptotic time average with positive probability. This one-sided no-reexpansion estimate would complete the uniqueness proof; it is not supplied by comparison, compact recurrence, or the finite positive-bulk contraction alone.

task_title: Final attack on ridge-specific noncommuting membrane insertion and last-step overlap
notes: No genuine counterexample within the positive affine ridge/membrane class was found. The problem is reduced to a sharp one-hyperplane insertion/renewal inequality: adding a semipermeable affine membrane of strength beta should cost at most e^{-2 beta}, even in the presence of previously inserted noncommuting affine membranes. This is proved in the one-dimensional and commuting cases; proving the noncommuting boundary-renewal maximum principle would yield the desired compact-local Hellinger criterion with explicit dimension-free constants.

task_title: Prove the ordered-pair one-sided local-time no-reexpansion estimate or find a genuine obstruction
notes: The t18 one-sided averaged reexpansion estimate is not merely unproved; in the singular/Jordan-positive-part form it is false. The finite positive-bulk contraction remains valid, but any completion of the monotone synchronization route must exploit signed heat-smoothed or negative-Sobolev cancellation of local-time differences rather than bounding the raw positive total variation.

task_title: Prove or disprove the noncommuting single-membrane insertion theorem and close Hellinger overlap
notes: The obstruction is sharper than noncommuting intersection strata: no old membranes are needed. The failure is caused by noncommutation between a tilted membrane and the anisotropic OU drift. Thus the membrane-insertion route in its stated same-time form cannot verify the compact-local Hellinger condition for the SPDE approximants; a weaker local, time-slowed, or differently normalized estimate would be needed.
